model_output_raw_text:
{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    pct_start = 0.15
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE * 4.0,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=pct_start,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, the current constant LR underuses early optimization. A one-cycle schedule reallocates learning-rate budget to accelerate early progress and anneal by the end, which is typically strong for short-horizon CIFAR-10 training while keeping code complexity low.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switching from Adam to AdamW usually improves short-budget generalization when combined with OneCycle LR, because decoupled weight decay regularizes more predictably under aggressive LR schedules.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The run is stable and improving; increasing base LR should make OneCycle’s peak LR more aggressive (from 2e-3 to 2.8e-3), which often improves short-horizon optimization on CIFAR-10 under a strict 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Validation loss keeps improving with optimization tweaks, so the next likely gain is modest capacity increase. Raising base channels from 12 to 16 should improve feature quality within 256 steps while keeping the model compact and stable.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After a strong gain from widening convolutional capacity, increasing the classifier bottleneck is a low-risk companion change that can better use richer features with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Recent large gains came from added capacity, suggesting the model is still underparameterized at this short horizon. A further moderate width increase should improve representational power while keeping the script simple and stable.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Width scaling continues to help, so adding one more pooling-conv stage is a plausible next capacity upgrade. Greater depth can improve feature abstraction and may reduce validation loss further within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After substantial capacity increases, mild dropout is a targeted regularization step to improve generalization without materially changing optimization dynamics or code complexity.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The dropout change caused a large regression, so reverting it is the highest-confidence move to recover the stronger no-dropout configuration for this short training budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After deepening the network, a slightly higher base LR can improve early progress under OneCycle and may better exploit the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase produced a regression, so reverting to 7e-4 restores the better-performing operating point for the current architecture and schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    pct_start = 0.3","old":"    pct_start = 0.15","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With the deeper network, extending the OneCycle warmup phase can stabilize early updates and often improves final loss under short fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"A slightly stronger decoupled weight decay is a simple regularization adjustment that can improve validation loss now that model capacity has been increased.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Weight decay increase to 2e-4 improved validation loss, so a small follow-up increase tests whether this larger model still benefits from stronger regularization under AdamW.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Increasing weight decay to 3e-4 regressed validation loss, so reverting to 2e-4 restores the stronger regularization setting found so far.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current deeper/wider backbone is strong; increasing the classifier bottleneck is a simple next capacity step that may better preserve and separate high-level features before the final logits.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Classifier width increases have been consistently beneficial; a final moderate bump to 128 is a straightforward way to test remaining head-capacity gains with minimal code change.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity scaling has been consistently effective; increasing backbone width from 20 to 24 is the highest-upside remaining simple change to improve representation quality within fixed steps.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Recent improvements continue from controlled capacity increases; raising the classifier width to 160 is a targeted low-complexity extension likely to extract more value from the stronger backbone.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The 160-unit head regressed substantially, so the final move is to revert to 128, which produced the best validation loss in this run.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}
